column3: Carlene‡This dataset has been built in the framework of the optimization of the MUlti-Source surveillance Tool for the detection of Avian Influenza outbreaks in mammalian species (MUST-AI). The MUST-AI tool collects health events reported from 3 sources:  two official sources
column4: WAHIS from the World Animal Health Organization and mails from Program for Monitoring Emerging Diseases (ProMED); and one unofficial source
column5: PADI-web
column6:
which collects online media articles. PADI-web uses 5 various strategies to locate health events mentioned in the text articles. The aim of our study was to assess the various strategies.
The dataset consists 7 case studies (outbreak events from official sources WAHIS or the scientific literature) associated to 222 validated media articles collected by PADI-web through the 5 strategies. The matching criteria to associate a case study to a PADI-web article are based on the country of the outbreak and the time period.
.
The five evaluated strategies are:.
(A) SpaCy locations in Outbreak articles: extraction with SpaCy of locations in articles classified as an epidemiological outbreak. .
(B) SpaCy locations in Outbreak articles and Current event sentences: extraction with SpaCy of locations in articles classified as an epidemiological outbreak and in a sentence that has been classified as relating to a current event..
(C) SpaCy locations in beginning of Outbreak articles: extraction with SpaCy of locations found in the first 300 characters of the text of an article classified as an epidemiological outbreak. .
(D) PADI-web-specific locations: extraction of locations by the location extraction model trained on PADI-web data..
(E) SpaCy locations in beginning of articles: extraction with SpaCy of locations found in the first 300 characters of the text of an article..
.
Each case study is associated with an identification number. For each case study
column7:
the set of Padiweb articles is given with a unique identification number. The dataset contains the values as follows: .
- Source: source of the case study
column8:
as WAHIS or published article in the scientific literature.
- Id_gold_standard: case study identification number as the outbreak id reported in WAHIS or ranked literature case study number.
- Id_article: identification number of media articles as generated in PADI-web - URL: url to the source article.
- Strategy X: binary value that stipulates whether the article has been returned by the strategy X.

column3:
Samantha‡This dataset contains 7390 near infrared spectra of 160 herbarium specimens of five species of the genus Pistacia.

The herbarium specimens cover a collection period from the 18th century to the present day and come from various countries around the Mediterranean.

The spectra were collected on 109 herbarium specimens and 51 new specimens over species of Pistacia. Herbarium specimens were sampled in the herbaria of the University of Montpellier and the MNHN in Paris. The 51 new specimens are herbarium samples kept at CIRAD Montpellier.

The 5 species included in the study are :
PIALE: Pistacia lentiscus L.
PIATE: Pistacia terebinthus L.
PIARA: Pistacia x saportae Burnat
PIAAT: Pistacia atlantica Desf.
PIAVE: Pistacia vera L.

The spectral data are available in two versions: The first is the raw file with all the raw spectra (30 to 50 spectra per sample) without any treatments. The second is pre-processed spectral data
column4:
ready to use without any specific spectral knowledge. The last file contains all the data associated with the herbarium specimens. We choose to add a set of pictures to illustrate our protocol.

column3: Caroline‡Les questions liées à « mobilité » confèrent un caractère clairement pluridisciplinaire en Sciences Sociales (migrations
column4: démographie
column5: etc.) mais également dans d’autres domaines comme la Santé (par exemple

column7: études liées à la mobilité animale pour l’utilisation des ressources alimentaires). Dans ce contexte
column8: il semble important de construire une terminologie qui puisse être à la fois spécifique aux domaines étudiés tout en respectant une certaine généricité. Dans ce contexte
column9:
des méthodes de fouille de textes peuvent être appliquées pour amorcer la construction d’une telle terminologie. Ces approches reposent sur l'utilisation de corpus qui sont décrits ci-dessous.

column1: https://doi.org/10.18167/DVN1/SGNSII‡https://doi.org/10.18167/DVN1/SGNSII‡https://doi.org/10.18167/DVN1/SGNSII‡file://10.18167/DVN1/SGNSII‡English‡doi:10.18167/DVN1/SGNSII‡2024-04-08T13:20:33Z‡2023-11-15T12:40:38Z‡‡High resolution map of plant available water content for Senegal
column2: derived from iSDA Africa 30m soil properties maps using USDA Rosetta3 model‡Lavarenne
column3: Jérémy‡Lavarenne
column4:
Jérémy‡Purpose:

The purpose of this dataset is to provide a high resolution map of available water content for Senegal and Gambia derived from 30m soil properties maps using the iSDA Africa dataset and the USDA Rosetta3 model. The map can be used to notably support spatialized crop simulation models to determine potential crop yields across West Africa.

Nature and extent of data:

The dataset consists of a high resolution map of plant available water content derived from four soil property datasets from iSDA Africa. The soil properties include sand
column5: clay
column6: silt and fine-earth bulk density
column7: and were predicted at a 30m resolution for 0-20 cm and 20-50cm depth intervals. The USDA Rosetta model was used to compute soil water retention properties from four iSDA layers (sand
column8: clay
column9: silt content
column10: and fine-earth bulk density)
column11: including the van Genuchten parameters of residual water content
column14: 'n' shape parameter
column15: and saturated hydraulic conductivity. From these parameters
column16: the volumetric water content at field capacity and permanent wilting point was computed using the van Genuchten-Mualem model. The output map has been filled with nearest neighbor interpolation where NaN values were present. Finally
column17:
available water content integrated over the soil profile down to 50cm and to the bedrock depth was calculated.

Location and coverage:

The dataset covers the countries of Senegal and Gambia in West Africa at a 30m resolution.

Temporal scope:

The dataset was created using soil property data and models from iSDA Africa and USDA Rosetta3
column18: respectively
column19:
and covers the temporal scope of the original datasets.
Archive formats / unzipping:

Some of the archive files are separated in multiple parts
column20: as indicated by their numeral file extensions (i.e. *.001
column23: please consider using an unzipping software such as 7zip
column24:
that will manage file concatenation and unzipping given that all parts are stored under the same folder in your file system.

column3: Lionel‡La cartographie du suivi de la récolte de la canne à sucre a été réalisée en 2020 sur toute la sole cannière de La Réunion. La cartographie présente les dates de début et de fin de récolte d’entités en canne à sucre obtenues par le croisement des couches de l’occupation du sol (Carte d’occupation du sol 2019 Spot-6/7 de niveau3)
column4: du Registre Parcellaire Graphique 2018 et des routes. La chaîne de traitement automatique MASH (Mapping Sugarcane Harvest) cartographie l’avancement de la récolte de canne à sucre en temps semi-réel. La méthode consiste à traiter automatiquement des images satellitaires libres (radar et optique) acquises à haute fréquence (moins de 6 jours). Chaque pixel des parcelles en canne à sucre est classé selon son état de récolte. 32 images satellites en optique (Sentinel-2A et 2B) et 14 images radar (Sentinel-1) du programme Copernicus de l’ESA ont été traitées. column3: Babacar‡The tree inventory was conducted in the Faidherbia albida parkland in Senegal
column4: in the region of Niakhar. A field campaign for tree species data collection was carried out in May 2018.  It is a weighted stratified sampling of 213 observation sites distributed according to a landscape heterogeneity gradient. At each site
column5: an exhaustive inventory of the trees in a 1 ha plot was carried out. In addition
column6: extra species (not present in the plot) observed within a radius of 400 m were included to increase the odds of recording all species in the area. Individual tree location and species were recorded with a Garmin GSMAP 64 GPS device. Due to a GPS-reported accuracy of 3 m
column7: the location of each individual tree was then adjusted by photointerpretation using Pleiades images (0.5 m spatial resolution). In all

column11:
based on existing field sources such as Google Maps and the GeoNames database

  • GeoNames Feature Class : The feature class corresponding to the GeoNames ID (empty if no match in GeoNames)
  • Type: A manually assigned type of spatial entity (country

column3: Flavie‡Reducing antibiotic use in livestock production has been a target for national action plans worldwide. The Vietnamese livestock plan issued in 2017 has
column4: among other objectives
column5: strengthened the regulatory framework for antibiotic use and has adopted new regulations. This study explores the level of understanding
column6: acceptance
column7:
and application of these regulations among veterinary drug value chain stakeholders. An iterative stakeholder mapping and analysis of the veterinary drug value chain was conducted in north and south Vietnam. We conducted a focus group discussion and 39 semi-structured interviews with stakeholders of the veterinary drug value chain. We identified 10 factors that could influence the implementation of the new regulations.

This dataset include 9/35 transcripts of the interviews for which it has been possible to de-identified totally the transcript. Supplementary materials included: 1: Guide for focus group discussion 2: Guide for semi-structured interviews 3: Classification of stakeholders 4: Factors influencing the implementation of new regulations‡CIRAD; antimicrobials; livestock production; stakeholder analysis; regulations; L70 - Sciences et hygiène vétérinaires - Considérations générales‡Text‡"classification-stakeholders.pdf; factors-regulations.pdf; guide_focus-group-discussion.pdf; guide_semi-structured-interviews.pdf; Interview-01.pdf; Interview-02.pdf; Interview-03.pdf; Interview-15.pdf; Interview-16.pdf; Interview-17.pdf; Interview-22.pdf; Interview-24.pdf; Interview-27.pdf; Interview-36.pdf; Interview-37.pdf; Interview-38.pdf; Interview-39.pdf"‡CC BY 4.0‡http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0‡CIRAD; antimicrobials; livestock production; stakeholder analysis; regulations; L70 - Sciences et hygiène vétérinaires - Considérations générales‡CIRAD; antimicrobials; livestock production; stakeholder analysis; regulations; L70 - Sciences et hygiène vétérinaires - Considérations générales‡‡‡‡‡‡

column4: Stéphane‡"Dans le cadre du Centre d’Expertise Scientifique Occupation des Sols de THEIA
column5:
l’UMR TETIS du CIRAD développe une méthode de cartographie automatisée fondée sur la chaine Moringa qui minimise les interactions avec les utilisateurs par l’automatisation de la plupart des processus d’analyse et de traitement des images.
La méthodologie utilise conjointement une image à Très Haute Résolution Spatiale (Spot6/7 ou Pléiades) et une ou plusieurs séries temporelles d’images optiques à Haute Résolution Spatiale type Sentinel-2 et Landsat-8 pour une classification combinant segmentation et classification objet (utilisation de l’algorithme Random Forest) entrainée par une base de données d’apprentissage constituée à partir de collecte in situ et de photo-interprétation.
Les cartes d'occupation du sol sont réalisées dans le cadre du projet GABIR (Gestion Agricole des Biomasses à l’échelle de l'Ile de la Réunion) et sont toutes diffusées sur le catalogue de données spatiales du Cirad : https://geode.cirad.fr/
Cette fiche du Dataverse concerne les cartes produites
column6: pour l'année 2017
column7: en utilisant une mosaïque d'images Spot6/7 pour calculer la segmentation (extraction d'objets homogènes à partir de l'image). Nous utilisons une base de données terrain ayant une nomenclature emboitée avec 3 niveaux de précision nous permettant de produire une classification par niveau. Le niveau le plus détaillé distinguant les types de cultures présente une précision globale de 86% et un indice de Kappa est de 0
column8: 87. Le niveau 2
column9: distinguant les groupes de cultures présente une précision globale de 93% et un indice de Kappa est de 0
column10: 94. Le niveau 1
column11: distinguant les grands groupes d'occupation du sol présente une précision globale de 98% et un indice de Kappa est de 0

Description: This dataset provides daily interpolated rainfall maps for Martinique island
column6: for the period 1980-2021
column7: at 0.001° resolution
column8:
derived from daily rain gauge data observations.

Dataset Preparation Methods: These maps were obtained by performing interpolation from daily rain gauge data (Martinique ORSTOM-DIREN dataset v1 (doi:10.5281/zenodo.10263910)
column9: using universal kriging with external drift from a digital elevation model
column10:
with fitting of Exponential variogram models. Resolution of interpolation is 0.001°. The daily results were assembled into yearly netCDF files.

column1: https://doi.org/10.18167/DVN1/R6LXYL‡https://doi.org/10.18167/DVN1/R6LXYL‡https://doi.org/10.18167/DVN1/R6LXYL‡file://10.18167/DVN1/R6LXYL‡English‡doi:10.18167/DVN1/R6LXYL‡2025-06-23T08:55:23Z‡2025-04-01T08:51:11Z‡‡Dataset for participatory process diagnosis on pounded yam product profile in Ivory Coast‡Bouniol
column3: Alexandre‡The quality of local consumer products in West Africa depends on numerous parameters that enable them to meet consumer expectations. To increase the chances of adoption of new genotypes developed by breeders
column4: it is necessary to increasingly deepen the identification of adoption criteria among users of these raw materials. Among the latter
column5: processors play a key role because they are at the interface between raw material producers and consumers; they therefore need varieties that are highly processable while still producing finished products of the quality expected by consumers. This participatory assessment of the pounded yam process into Foufou (pounded dough product) aimed
column6: through the processing of ten contrasting varieties
column7: to i) describe in detail the processing process
column8: particularly the cooking and pounding steps; ii) compare the pounding ability of yam varieties based on drudgery criteria.  In addition to a detailed description of the processing process
column9: this study highlighted i) the importance of the dry matter content of the raw material
column10: which must not be less than 28%; ii) the different pounding behaviors between varieties
column11: with workloads that can vary by as much as twofold depending on the varieties used. This study enabled the development of new approaches to quantify the laboriousness of unit operations through video analysis. The first results presented here will need to be consolidated through additional studies on new genotypes while testing new participatory methods for describing the process of obtaining Foufou.‡CIRAD; Yams; Foufou; Participatory research; labour; Pounded yam; Q - Food science and technology‡Dataset‡"RTB Breeding Participatory_Process_diagnosis_Dataset_Method_Pounded_Yam_CIRAD_IVORY_COAST_28032025.xlsx"‡CC BY-NC-SA 4.0‡http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0‡CIRAD; Yams; Foufou; Participatory research; labour; Pounded yam; Q - Food science and technology‡CIRAD; Yams; Foufou; Participatory research; labour; Pounded yam; Q - Food science and technology‡English‡‡‡‡‡

column5: Valentine‡"This work was conducted before the launch of ESA Sentinel-2 mission
column6: which images are particularly adapted to crop monitoring and characterization thanks to their high spatial (10 – 60m) and temporal (5 days) resolutions. column12: the objective of the CIRAD – TETIS joint research unit is to develop a mapping approach adapted to southern cropping systems constraints (small field size

column17:

These values are given by the Random Forest algorithm.

column2: Julien‡This dataset comprises data from a systematic review done after a comprehensive literature search conducted in January 2023 to identify peer-reviewed and grey literature on the impact of agroforestry on soil organic carbon (SOC) in Central America
column3: the Caribbean
column4: and specific regions of South America (Colombia
column5: Peru
column6: Ecuador
column7: and Venezuela). We used the following academic databases: Web of Science Core Collection
column8: Scopus

column11: www.ovid.com). We also included Tthe institutional database of the Centro Agronómico Tropical de Investigación y Enseñanza (CATIE
column12: https://repositorio.catie.ac.cr/) was also included to expand the search with grey literature (PhD and MSc thesis).  This database compiles the results of 60 studies
column13: 311 paired observations of SOC storage rates and 332 response ratios (RR) from 44 and 54 field studies conducted between 1986 and 2022. From included studies
column14: we extracted SOC data for both agroforestry plots and their corresponding control plots. We captured mean values
column15: indicators of dispersion (standard error [SE] or standard deviation [SD])
column16: and the number of replicates for both treatment and control groups.  Our database represents
column17: to our knowledge the widest and most rigorous analysis of available data on the subject. This database can help bridging the knowledge gap on the role of agroforestry in soil carbon storage in the Latin America and the Caribbean (LAC) region. It also helps identifying key drivers of SOC storage variability and provide insights for developing effective carbon management strategies in agroforestry systems. column3: Stéphane‡Dupuy
column4:
Stéphane‡As part of THEIA (the French Data and Services center for continental surfaces) CIRAD's TETIS research unit is developing an automated mapping method based on the Moringa chain that minimizes interactions with users by automating most image analysis and processing.
The methodology uses jointly a Very High Spatial Resolution image (Spot6/7 or Pleiades) and one or more time series of High Spatial Resolution optical images such as Sentinel-2 and Landsat-8 for a classification combining segmentation and object classification (use of the Random Forest algorithm) driven by a learning database constituted from in situ collection and photo-interpretation.
The land use maps are produced as part of the GABIR project (Gestion Agricole des Biomasses à l'échelle de l'Ile de la Réunion) and are all distributed on CIRAD's spatial data catalogue : https://geode.cirad.fr
This Dataverse entry concerns the maps produced
column5: for the year 2017
column6: using a mosaic of Pleiades images to calculate segmentation (extraction of homogeneous objects from the image). We use a field database with a nested nomenclature with 3 levels of accuracy allowing us to produce a classification by level. The most detailed level distinguishing crop types has an overall accuracy of 86% and a Kappa index of 0.85. Level 2
column7: distinguishing crop groups
column8: has an overall accuracy of 92% and a Kappa index of 0.90. Level 1
column9: distinguishing major land use groups
column10: has an overall accuracy of 97% and a Kappa index of 0.94. column3:
Raphaël‡"This dataset contains:

- simulation outputs of a light model performed on various design of oil palm with variations in planting density (inter row and intra row distances). The data are the transmitted light to the ground. Light simulations were performed using the ARCHIMED-PHYS software (https://archimed-platform.github.io/).

- phenological
column4: morphological
column5: physiological
column6: and productive traits including physicochemical traits were measured in phytotrons (Abiophen platform of CIRAD) on eight contrasted rice varieties. The three light treatments were set up in three independent growth chambers
column7:
respectively:
• a control treatment (C) following the dynamic of incident light observed in Sumatra (with a ceiling radiation value that can be reached i‧e 1100 µmolphotons m-2 s-1)
• the agroforestry shading regime corresponding to the planting design selected from the simulation study (S-AF)
• a uniform shading treatment (S) with the same amount of incident light as S-AF

This dataset was used for the paper 'Combining modelling and experimental approaches to assess the feasibility of developing rice-oil palm agroforestry system' (https://doi.org/10.1101/2022.12.19.521043). Full description of the measurements and light treatments are presented in the material & method section of the paper.

The data/baobabs/ folder includes the occurrence dataset for the seven baobab species present in Madagascar (file data_Adansonia‧csv). This file is the result of years of field inventories in Madagascar by botanists and ecologists working at CIRAD.

A development version of this code is available on GitHub: https://github.com/ghislainv/baobabs_mada.

column3: Cécile N‡"Dataset of the paper entitled ""Phosphorus sorption and availability in an andosol after a decade of organic or mineral fertilizer applications: importance of pH and organic carbon modifications in soil as compared to phosphorus accumulation"". We conducted a 10-years-old field experiment on an andosol and compared fields that had been amended with mineral or organic (dairy slurry and manure compost) fertilizers against a non-fertilized control. Water and Olsen extractions and inorganic phosphorus sorption experiments were realized on soils sampled after 6 and 10 years of trial. column3: Emmanuel‡The CNRA yam genetic resources collection contains a diversity of accessions consisting of traditional
column4: introduced and hybrid varieties. This collection was the subject of characterization of the quality of pounded yam (foutou) in order to identify varieties suitable for making pounded yam. The sensory parameters measured are the extensogram peak force (N)
column5: extensibility (mm)
column6: extension area (k.mm). The results show that the measured parameters are positively and strongly correlated. Among these parameters
column7: the extension area (k.mm) best discriminates the two species and extensibility the genotypes. PCA coupled with ascending hierarchical classification (ACH) made it possible to screen the varieties into three classes taking into account their extensibility
column8: that is to say their ability to make pounded yam that pulls. Class 1 consists of 10 varieties with high extensibility. The varieties in this class
column9: all of the species D. rotundata
column10: are capable of making good pounded yam and can therefore be selected. Class 2 includes 21 varieties with medium extensibility. The varieties in this class
column11: which include both D. rotundata and D. alata varieties
column12: can make foutou of medium quality. Class 3 includes varieties of D. rotundata

Ce jeu de données est le résultat de trois études ont porté sur l’élevage camelin dans différentes zones arides du Maroc (Zagora
column4: Guelmim-Oued Noun
column5: M’hamid El Ghizlane et Tagounit)
column6: dans le cadre des travaux de l’Institut Agronomique et Vétérinaire Hassan II
column7:
en partenariat avec le CIRAD et d’autres institutions.

Compte tenu de la dispersion géographique et de la mobilité des éleveurs
column8: l’échantillonnage a été réalisé selon la méthode “boule de neige”
column9: en collaboration avec les services vétérinaires (ONSSA)
column10: les autorités locales ou les chefs de tribus. Les campagnes de collecte se sont déroulées entre mars et mai
column11:
sur des périodes variant de 45 jours à deux mois selon les mémoires.

Les variables recueillies concernent la structure et la taille des troupeaux (sexe
column14: composition) et les paramètres démographiques (taux de naissance
column17:
exploitation).

Les données sont présentées sous la forme d'une base Access structurée en 3 onglets :

  • - T_HERD décrit des fermes d'élevage de camélidés au Maroc
column18: avec des informations administratives et temporelles
column19:
mais sans données géolocalisées précises. L'onglet contient 305 entrées (lignes) et 19 colonnes.
  • - T_Q1 détaille les caractéristiques reproductives et démographiques des camélidés dans les fermes
column20:
avec une forte proportion de femelles et des événements reproductifs peu fréquents. L'onglet contient 15 154 entrées (lignes) et 15 colonnes.
  • - T_Q2_1 décrit les événements d'entrée (principalement des achats) de camélidés dans les fermes
column22: le sexe et la valeur d'entrée
column23: mais avec de nombreuses données manquantes sur la race
column25: le lieu et le vendeur. column4: a LabSpecPro (Analytical Spectral Devices Inc.
column5: Boulder
column6: Colorado
column7: USA). The wavelength range was from 350 nm to 2500 nm
column8:
with a resolution of 1 nm. The probe used for reflectance measurements had a diameter of 4 mm. Each recorded spectrum was the average of 30 repeated measurements.

The herbarium collection used for this work was established in 2017. It was based on two different experiments in natural savannah in Northern Senegal. In this area
column9:
the savannah is composed of two different layers of vegetation: annual herbaceous species mostly grasses and perennial woody species. Samples were collected in enclosed plots not impacted by grazing

The collection used contained 308 samples 958 samples of 31 species
column10: mounted on Canson paper. A majority of the samples contained stem and leaves and few samples also presented reproductive organs at various phenological stages (flower and fruits). We repeated several measurements on the samples. We performed different series of measurements per type of organ: leaves
column11: stems and reproductive organs when they were available (flowers or fruits). We used 20 measurement points per sample and per organ. Moreover
column13: we took measurements from the bottom to the top of the plant
column14:
in order to cover within-sample diversity and obtain a gradient of organ development.

The Data are on Excel File with three sheets. The main sheet contains 2153 columns. The first column is a species code (Xspe) the list of species is on separate sheet
column15: the second column is the organ (F: reproductive flower and fruit
column16: L: leaves; S Steam) and the third one is a code for the sample code (most of the herbarium collection are available in the CIRAD Montpellier)
column17: The last 2150 columns are the reflectance’s in the wavelengths from 350-2500nm with one column per nm. column2: Denis‡Near infrared spectrometry allows the measurement of the absorbance or reflectance of the infrared region of the electromagnetic spectrum of a sample whose composition one wishes to determine in part. If the principle is common to the various applications
column3: the spectrum produced depends on the specific conditions of measurement and more particularly on the sample (e‧g. granulometry
column4: moisture content)
column5: the environment (e‧g. temperature)
column6: the spectrometer (e‧g. model
column7: brand
column8: age) but also on the interaction between these 3 types of variables. If it is possible to minimize the influence of the product and the environment (i‧e. by standardizing the sample preparation and measurement conditions)
column9: the spectrum remains the product of the sample/spectrometer pair. This dependence of the calibration on the spectrometer on which the measurements have been performed limits the generality of the results and the possibilities of data exchange in the network. It imposes a fastidious work of standardization associated with a loss of precision as soon as the spectrometer park evolves
column10: that the latter gets old or that the predictive model is used on another spectrometer
column11: even from the same manufacturer. In order to increase the generality of the results
column12: it is necessary to compare the evolution of the spectra of the same products on several spectrometers. The specific objective was to create and fill in a standard multi-machine and multi-product database in near infrared spectroscopy. Wood collection (xylotheque)

CIRAD's xylotheque is one of the most important wood collections of tropical and temperate species available to date. Initiated in 1937 from several small collections that have been gathered since the end of the 19th century for exhibition and demonstration purposes
column7: it is now regularly updated and enriched
column8:
particularly in the context of exchanges of specimens with various scientific partner at an international level.

The collection consists of more than 34
column9: 000 wood samples corresponding to about 8
column10: 400 species
column12: 155 genera and 247 botanical families. The collection
column14: encompasses the collection of wood stricto sensu (standard size board 13 x 6 x 1 cm and specimens of various sizes and shapes)
column16: herbaria and ancillary products (mostly duplicates and falls). It is computerized and managed through a database
column17:
which includes all the descriptors of wood samples and anatomical slides.

column3: Flavie‡Antimicrobial resistance is one of the major public health issues and solutions to reduce its usage are urgently needed. column3: Alexandre‡The quality of local consumer products in West Africa depends on numerous parameters that enable them to meet consumer expectations. To increase the chances of adoption of new genotypes developed by breeders
column4: it is necessary to increasingly deepen the identification of adoption criteria among users of these raw materials. Among the latter
column5: processors play a key role because they are at the interface between raw material producers and consumers; they therefore need varieties that are highly processable while still producing finished products of the quality expected by consumers. This participatory assessment of the plantain and cassava processing process into Foutou (pounded dough product) aimed
column6: through the processing of six contrasting varieties
column7: to i) describe in detail the processing process
column8:
particularly the cooking and pounding steps; ii) compare the pounding ability of plantain varieties based on drudgery criteria; and iii) characterize the finished products based on one of their essential textural properties: their extensibility.
In addition to a detailed description of the processing process
column9: this study highlighted i) the importance of the dry matter content of the raw material
column10: which must not be less than 30%; ii) the different pounding behaviors between varieties
column11: with workloads that can vary by as much as twofold depending on the varieties used. This study enabled the development of new approaches to quantify the laboriousness of unit operations through video analysis. The first results presented here will need to be consolidated through additional studies on new genotypes while testing new participatory methods for describing the process of obtaining Foutou.‡CIRAD; participatory research; foufou; cassava; labour; dry matter content; plantains; Q - Food science and technology‡Dataset‡"RTB Breeding Participatory_Process_diagnosis_Dataset_Method_FOUTOU_CIRAD_IVORY_COAST_28032025.xlsx"‡CC BY-NC-SA 4.0‡http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0‡CIRAD; participatory research; foufou; cassava; labour; dry matter content; plantains; Q - Food science and technology‡CIRAD; participatory research; foufou; cassava; labour; dry matter content; plantains; Q - Food science and technology‡English‡‡‡‡‡

column2: Ephraim‡Cassava utilization for food and/or industrial products depends on inherent root dry matter content (DMC). Accordingly
column3: there is need to develop NIRS models to aid breeding and selection of dry matter content as a critical industrial trait taking care of root sample preparation and cassava germplasm diversity available in Uganda. For calibration development (300 clones) were used. Grating was the main sample preparation method. Reference DMC data was obtained by oven-drying.  DMC in calibration samples ranged from 20 to 45%. Modified Partial least square (MPLS) regression model was used with the pretreatment carried out by SNVD over the segment 1100-2500nm at a 2nm step. The number of factors used was 8 while a total number of 13 outliers were eliminated. The regression coefficients (R2) of 0.997 were obtained while the R2 for cross validation was 0.993. In addition
column4: the RMSEC was 0.28 while the RMSECV was 0.43. These findings show the robustness of NIRS in estimation of dry matter content and thus justify its use in routine cassava breeding operations.‡CIRAD; cassava; dry matter; NIRS; infrared spectrophotometry; grating; food analysis; spectral data; Food science and Technology‡Dataset‡"WP3_NaCRRI_fresh_Grated_Cassava_DM.tab"‡CC BY-NC-SA 4.0‡http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0‡CIRAD; cassava; dry matter; NIRS; infrared spectrophotometry; grating; food analysis; spectral data; Food science and Technology‡CIRAD; cassava; dry matter; NIRS; infrared spectrophotometry; grating; food analysis; spectral data; Food science and Technology‡English‡‡‡‡‡

column3: Stéphane‡"We publish two land cover maps of the city of Antananarivo produced with data acquired in 2022 using a methodology combining machine learning and object-based image analysis (OBIA). This work follows on from work that resulted in a map of the same area in 2017. The maps are produced by processing satellite images using the Moringa processing chain developed in our lab. We use a Pleiades very high spatial resolution (VHSR) image
column4: a time series of Sentinel-2 images
column5:
a digital terrain model (DTM) and a reference database.
The Pleiades image is used to generate a layer of objects using a segmentation algorithm. Each object is then classified using the variables from the THRS image
column6: the time series and the DTM information. The hierarchical nomenclature used consists of four levels with a number of classes ranging from 4 to 19. We only publish here the most detailed map (level 4) which contains
column7: however
column8: in the attribute table
column9:
the information of the other levels.
The overall accuracy of the maps ranges from 93% to 84%. Such land cover products are very rare in Madagascar
column10:
so we decided to make them open access so that they can be used by land managers and researchers.
Warning
column11: since December 2
column12:
2022 we publish a new version to limit the effects related to flooding (the wetland class was overestimated on the previous version).
  • The map entitled ""version 1"" is produced with the SRTM digital surface model (DSM) with a spatial resolution of 30m.
  • The map entitled ""version 2"" is produced with a DTM and a LiDAR DSM with a spatial resolution of 1m (over a reduced area)

column3:
Pascal‡Estimation d'un risque de ruissellement vers les cours d'eau du Domaine Public Fluvial de l'île de La Réunion selon des pentes supérieures ou égales à 7%.

Le calcul de risque de ruissellement est effectué de manière itérative
column4: en partant des cours d'eau et en progressant le long des pentes jusqu'à une distance de 200m de part et d'autre de ces cours d'eau. Le calcul est effectué sur une grille de cellules de 5m de résolution spatiale. A chaque itération
column5: on calcule la pente par rapport aux points voisins déjà explorés et si cette pente est supérieure ou égale à 7% on marque les cellules concernées. Si la pente est inférieure
column6:
la progression à partir de ces cellules ne va pas plus loin.

Ce que l'on obtient est une estimation de ce risque de ruissellement qui n'est basée que sur les différences d'altitudes entre les points. Il n'est donc pas tenu compte de la couverture du sol
column7: de sa capacité d'infiltration
column8:
de sa rugosité ou autres facteurs de surface pouvant affecter ce ruissellement.

Ces calculs on été réalisés à l'aide d'un programme écrit en langage Ocelet.‡CIRAD; agricultural runoff; risk; ruissellement agricole; risque; Ocelet; process produced data; Environnement; Géographie; Geography‡Dataset‡"risque_pente7pc_ile.tif"‡CC BY-NC 4.0‡http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/4.0‡CIRAD; agricultural runoff; risk; ruissellement agricole; risque; Ocelet; process produced data; Environnement; Géographie; Geography‡CIRAD; agricultural runoff; risk; ruissellement agricole; risque; Ocelet; process produced data; Environnement; Géographie; Geography‡French‡-21.39286987301411‡-20.867704509301088‡55.83696348502094‡55.2166190559702‡

column2: Agnès‡Cell walls in plants are mainly composed of polysaccharides. Among them
column3: pectin
column4: cellulose and hemicellulose are composed of various sugars residues (acidic: galacturonic acid in pectins; or neutral: rhamnose
column5: galactose
column6: arabinose
column7: xylose
column8: glucose in cellulose
column9: hemicellulose and pectin side chains). This fine composition impacts the structure but also the cooking behavior and mechanical properties of cell walls. This method seeks to determine the cell wall composition (neutral sugars composition and galacturonic acid content) of destarched AIS (Alcohol Insoluble Solid) extracted from raw and cooked yam and banana. It is applicable for AIS extracted from every fruit or vegetable product and containing a low amount of residual starch. For RTB products a destarching step is mandatory before analysis. First the samples are hydrolyzed in order to obtain sugar residues. Pectin and hemicellulose are degraded into their individual neutral and acidic sugars by a simple acid hydrolysis (hot concentrated sulphuric acid). In contrast
column10: cellulose has to be solubilized under harsher conditions. For this reason
column11: a double hydrolysis is performed. Afterwards
column12: the free neutral sugars are derivatized into volatile alditol acetates before their separation in Gas Chromatography with a Flamme Ionization Detector (GC-FID). They are then quantified thanks to an internal standard of inositol. Whereas free galacturonic acid
column14: is quantified by colorimetry after reaction with 3-phenylphenol (MHDP) which produces a fuschia pink complex with a maximum absorption at 520 nm. This SOP could be applied in all labs working on cell wall and pectins.‡CIRAD; cell wall polysaccharides; pectin; neutral sugars composition; galacturonic acid; arabinose; galactose; rhamnose; food analysis; Food science and Technology‡Other‡"RTBfoods_SOP_Analysis of cell wall polysaccharides_Yam & plantain.pdf"‡CC BY-NC-SA 4.0‡http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0‡CIRAD; cell wall polysaccharides; pectin; neutral sugars composition; galacturonic acid; arabinose; galactose; rhamnose; food analysis; Food science and Technology‡CIRAD; cell wall polysaccharides; pectin; neutral sugars composition; galacturonic acid; arabinose; galactose; rhamnose; food analysis; Food science and Technology‡English‡‡‡‡‡

column3: François‡The overall objective of the ASSET Project is to transform food and agricultural systems in Southeast Asia more sustainable
column4: safer and inclusive
column5: through harnessing the potential of Agroecology.  In this context
column6:
a joint workshop ASSET-ALiSEA was organized by GRET and CIRAD in Cambodia the 24th of November 2022. An agroecology lexicon composed of 20 concepts and 261 terms was constructed in the context of this workshop. This lexicon could be used for the knowledge hub associated with the ASSET Project.
This dataset contains:
- A presentation that summarizes the content of this workshop and the method used to construct these agroecology lexicons
- The agroecology theoretical lexicon (TL) with terms validated
- The agroecology practical lexicon (PL) co-constructed in Cambodia
Results:
- Theoretical lexicon (TL): 16 concepts with 331 terms - 210 terms manually validated in the Workshop

column3: Eric‡In tropical countries subject to significant quantitative and qualitative variations in the availability of fodder during the year
column4:
the dissatisfaction of feed needs is a frequent situation for animals maintained on rangelands without a sufficient supplementation with feed concentrates.
The Body Condition Scoring (BCS) is a useful way to assess the state of body reserves (subcutaneous fat
column5: muscle mass) which reflects the animal's production (growth
column6: milk
column7: work) and reproduction capacities. The evaluation of the body reserves of an animal through the scoring of their body condition is important to adjust their diet and appreciate their general state of health
column8: as well as their reproductive and production capacity (meat
column9: milk
column10: work
column11: etc.). Managing body reserves is one way of responding to variability in quality and access to feed resources. In females
column12: reserves play an important buffer role during lactation. They can make up for insufficient intakes from the ration. Indeed
column13: the level of production depends on the nutrients provided by the feeds
column14: but also on the animal's body reserves when the feed-based supplies do not fully cover their needs
column15: especially during the dry season. The BCS impacts the interval between births. Overly lean females show a delay in the return of heat after giving birth
column16:
the direct consequence of which is the increase in the parturition interval and consequently a decrease in herd productivity.
The assessment of these reserves through Body Condition Scoring (BCS) represents a management tool for livestock farmers
column17: agricultural advisors and livestock development stakeholders. BCS is a simple
column18: inexpensive and fast method. Several animals can be scored in one session. It allows to compare the BCS of individuals or herds: 1) raised in different production systems or environments
column19: 2) or
column20: during different seasons (dry season and rainy season). BCS can be used as a tool for monitoring and alerting the nutritional level of domestic animal populations. To do this
column21:
BCS alert thresholds and a BCS collection and monitoring system must be defined on reasoned samples of animal populations.
It is an easy-to-use field tool. However
column22: a good mastery of the BCS grid as well as a regular practice of scoring are necessary to obtain precise and reproducible ratings. It can also be used as a tool for monitoring and alerting the nutritional level of animal populations. Thus
column23:
the farmer can be called at any time to intervene on the feed ration and / or the health of the animal.
Few studies have been carried out for developing a BCS grid
column24: specially devoted and applicable to Sub-Saharan goats in Africa. Existing BCS systems for goats are based on a four-points BCS grid (Imadine
column26: which has been adapted from systems previously developed for temperate goats (Morand-Fehr and Branca
column27: 1987; Hervieu et al.
column28: 1991). Such a system includes examining the lumbar region (transverse and spinous apophyses
column31: the sternal region (fat thickness) and the general appearance of the animal (ribs
column32: bones protrusions in the rump
column33: transverse and spinous apophyses; Imadine
column36:
1994). The criteria retained by those authors includes observation from the rear and from the flank of the animal.
In 2020
column37: Vall proposed a standardized BCS scoring system for tropical livestock animals for large animals (cattle
column41: and horses. column3:
Ernest Ogbete‡Cassava (Manihot esculenta Crantz) is a major root crop in the tropics and its starchy roots are significant sources of calories for more than 500 million people worldwide especially Nigeria. Cassava breeders aim to produce many clones rapidly with desired agronomic traits.

Near-infrared spectroscopy (NIRS) is a physical detection method that was developed in recent decades. This technique is based on the correlation between chemical properties and the absorption of light at specific wavelengths in the near-infrared region. A rapid and accurate method for determining dry matter (DM) content of cassava root will be valuable and help the breeders in selecting clones with good traits. This database contains the spectra of fresh intact cassava roots harvested at NRCRI Umudike Nigeria (between August and September 2020) with the use of Portable/handheld NIRS (Quality SpecTrek).

A total number of 1764 spectra were acquired from 98 (ninety-eight) clones of fresh intact cassava roots. Three different sizes of cassava roots (big
column4: medium and small sized) were cut in horizontal positions C1 (proximal)
column5: C3 (central)
column6: and C5 (distal). Calibration model developed for dry matter content (DM) showed the mean and standard deviation to be 39.44 and 4.73 respectively with the standard error of laboratory at 0.76. An SOP on the protocol for the determination of DM on fresh intact cassava root using a handheld NIRS has been linked to this database. The ASD hand-held NIRS was acquired by NRCRI (National Root Crops Research Institute) Umudike Nigeria in the year 2016.‡CIRAD; cassava; flours; starch; dry matter; NIRS; infrared spectrophotometry; food analysis; Food science and Technology‡Dataset‡"RTBfoods_K.2.47B_Spectral database_NIRS_Starch & DM_Cassava Flour_NRCRI Nigeria_2021.tab"‡‡‡CIRAD; cassava; flours; starch; dry matter; NIRS; infrared spectrophotometry; food analysis; Food science and Technology‡CIRAD; cassava; flours; starch; dry matter; NIRS; infrared spectrophotometry; food analysis; Food science and Technology‡English‡‡‡‡‡

column6: Anissa‡This dataset consists of supplementary data from the paper evaluating a public-private partnership (PPP) in the veterinary sector in Tunisia: the Sanitary Mandate. The evaluation was conducted remotely from March to June 2021. It includes an Excel spreadsheet containing the analysis of interviews conducted during the evaluation
column7:
capturing responses from different groups of stakeholders involved in the PPP.

The database is structured to support the qualitative evaluation of collaboration within the PPP and includes analysed data from semi-structured interviews with various actors at both regional and national levels. The analysis focuses on the historical and contextual elements of the evaluated PPP as well as the collaborative processes between public and private actors.

column8: a supplementary Word document accompanies this dataset

Appendix 3: Results of the historical and contextual analysis of the evaluated PPP.
